statement.p: Plot the line with equation y=\frac{2}{5}(x-1)+4\text{.}

solution.p: We can see from the equation that (1,4) is a point on the line. The first step in making a plot is to go to that point on the graph. We also see the slope is \frac{2}{5}\text{,} so from our launch point of (1,4)\text{,} we can move forward 5 and up 2 to find a second point on the line.
